fields.description:
## Context
Ruby and JavaScript/TypeScript projects using the DSO test gate today receive no signal from record-test-status.sh and record-test-exemption.sh because both scripts hardcode python3 -m pytest — RSpec and Jest tests are silently skipped. reinstall-hooks.sh also hard-fails on missing pyproject.toml, preventing hook installation on non-Python projects. This epic introduces a commands.test_runner config key so the test gate pipeline is stack-agnostic.

## Success Criteria
- commands.test_runner config key defined and documented in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md; onboarding auto-detects stack (Jest for Node/JS, RSpec for Ruby, pytest for Python) and writes the key during setup
- record-test-status.sh routes .rb, .js, .ts files to commands.test_runner instead of hardcoded python3/pytest
- record-test-exemption.sh uses commands.test_runner instead of hardcoded python3 -m pytest "$NODE_ID"
- reinstall-hooks.sh removes the hard-fail on missing pyproject.toml; installs hooks on any supported stack
- INSTALL.md updated with per-stack test runner optional dependency notes (Jest, RSpec, pytest)
- Validation: run onboarding on a Ruby project; commands.test_runner = bundle exec rspec written to dso-config.conf; record-test-status.sh against a .rb file records result using RSpec, not pytest

## Dependencies
None (first in the polyglot hardening sequence)

## Approach
Add commands.test_runner as a new config key read by the test gate pipeline. Onboarding detects the project stack and writes the appropriate default. All three gate scripts are updated to read the config key rather than hardcode Python tool invocations.
